title: De-identify PHI before analytics or non-clinical use
description: Strip or transform identifiers before patient data flows into analytics, test, or model-training environments so secondary use does not expose individuals.
body: Use when patient data leaves the clinical system for analytics, lower environments, or model training. Do not copy raw PHI into test/analytics stores, and avoid weak masking that leaves re-identifiable quasi-identifiers. Actions: remove or transform direct identifiers, generalize or suppress quasi-identifiers that enable re-identification, and verify the result against a de-identification standard before export; record the method and fields transformed. Validation signals include a de-identification review and a re-identification risk check on the output dataset. Risk boundary: advisory only; whether a dataset is adequately de-identified is a human privacy/compliance decision, so escalate rather than declaring data safe to share, and never relax de-identification to ease analytics.
